revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.

== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.

== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "下毛野氏}}" to "下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}"
revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "御諸別王}}" to "御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}"
revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.

== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.comment: removing bad jawiki links
revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡}}. Numerous large burial mounds (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's worship site is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)
