revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.

== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= <!-- Image for family crest --> |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=draft|12=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=draft|12=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=draft|12=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=draft|12=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft|12=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=draft|12=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=draft|12=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=draft|12=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=draft|12=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名=上毛野国造 <!-- |家紋= 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称= |本姓=[[上毛野氏]] |家祖=[[豊木入日子命]] |種別=[[皇別]]（異説あり） |出身地=[[大和国]] |根拠地=[[上毛野国]] |人物=[[#人物]]参照 |支流=[[下毛野国造]]、[[浮田国造]]、[[能等国造]]、[[針間鴨国造]] }} '''上毛野国造'''（かみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・かみつけぬこくぞう・かみつけののくにのみやつこ・かみつけのこくぞう）は[[上毛野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと毛野国造で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[下毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''上毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''上毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』では'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野・下毛野君の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の'''豊城入彦命'''が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の'''[[彦狭嶋王]]'''が[[景行|景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」では崇神朝の皇子・'''豊城入彦命'''の孫の'''彦狭嶋命'''が、はじめて東方の十二国を治め平らげて、国造に封ぜられたとされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[上毛野氏]]'''（かみつけのうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[朝臣]]を賜った。東国統治を担当し、しばしば[[蝦夷]]と交戦した。
[[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、大和から派遣された、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦|豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[大国主神|出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''のくに）を形成し、その後毛野国は上毛野国と[[下毛野国造|下毛野国]]（[[下野国]]）に分割された。古来より上毛野国は[[東京湾|江戸湾]]に注ぐ古[[利根川]]流域であったが[[利根川東遷事業]]により毛野川を合わせ大海・[[太平洋]]に注ぐ利根川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[群馬県]]とほぼ同じである。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は[[山田郡 (群馬県)|山田郡]]、[[新田郡]]周辺と見られる。両郡には大型古墳が多数築造されており、これらが国造の奥津城と見られる。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''上毛野国'''と呼ばれた地域で、後の[[律令国家]]の[[上野国]]、現在の[[群馬県]]にあたる。
== 氏神 == 国造の奉斎社は[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]に鎮座する上野国二宮の'''[[赤城神社]]'''か。祭神は国造の祖である[[大己貴命]]・[[豊城入彦命]]。
=== 墓 === *[[藤本観音山古墳]] *:[[栃木県]][[足利市]]にある全長117メートルの[[前方後方墳]]で、[[4世紀]]中頃の築造。被葬者は[[御諸別王]]か<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第六章 諸国の大古墳の被葬者」『巨大古墳と古代王統譜』青垣出版、2005年。</ref>。 *[[朝子塚古墳]] *:群馬県[[太田市]]にある全長123メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀末から[[5世紀]]初頭の築造。被葬者は御諸別王の妻か。 *[[別所茶臼山古墳]] *:群馬県太田市にある全長164メートルの前方後円墳で、4世紀末から5世紀初頭の築造。被葬者は[[荒田別]]か。 *[[太田天神山古墳]] *:群馬県太田市にある全長210メートルの前方後円墳で、5世紀前半から中頃の築造。被葬者は[[竹葉瀬]]か。
== 人物 == *'''[[御諸別王]]'''（みもろわけのみこ） *:[[古墳時代]]の官人。[[景行天皇]]朝に毛野へ赴く。 *'''[[荒田別|大荒田別命]]'''（おおあらたわけのみこと） *:古墳時代の武人。[[神功皇后]]摂政期に活動。 *'''[[竹葉瀬]]'''（たかはせ） *:古墳時代の武人。[[仁徳天皇]]朝の遣新羅使。白鹿を発見して天皇に献上したという。 *'''[[上毛野田道]]'''（かみつけののたぢ） *:古墳時代の武人。仁徳天皇朝の将軍。新羅を討つが、蝦夷に敗死したという。竹葉瀬の弟で[[下毛野国造]]の祖。 *'''[[上毛野小熊]]'''（かみつけののおぐま） *:古墳時代の武人。[[安閑天皇]]朝の国造。関東地方に覇をなし、[[武蔵国造]]家の内紛に介入したという。
== 子孫 == *[[上毛野形名]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の武人。[[舒明天皇]]朝の将軍。一度は蝦夷に敗れたが、妻の機転で巻き返したという。 *[[上毛野稚子]] *:飛鳥時代の武人。[[天智天皇]]朝の将軍。[[阿倍比羅夫]]らとともに新羅の城を落とした。 *[[上毛野三千]] *:飛鳥時代の官人。[[天武天皇]]朝の学者。[[川島皇子|川嶋皇子]]らと史書編纂に携わった。 *[[上毛野穎人]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。遣唐録司。東宮学士。[[薬子の変]]で上皇側の情報を密告した。 *[[上毛野滋子]] *:平安時代の女官。典侍。従三位。[[藤原良房]]家の人。 *[[上毛野永世]] *:平安時代の官人。尾張介。従四位下。[[貞観格]]編纂に参加した。
== 系譜 ==
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|かみつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:上野国|かみつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:群馬県の歴史|かみつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:国造|かみつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:上毛野氏|*かみつけの]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[上毛野国造]] to [[Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|qq=}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|qq=}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|qq=}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsandō|qq=}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|qq=}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|qq=}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nitta District|ja|新田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitta District|qq=}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|qq=}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi, Gunma|qq=}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|qq=}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|qq=}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|qq=}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|qq=}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|qq=}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|qq=}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|qq=}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|qq=}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|qq=}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi|ja|キミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|qq=}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi, Gunma|qq=}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|ja|藤本観音山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|太田天神山古墳|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|qq=}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|qq=}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|qq=}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|qq=}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|qq=}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|qq=}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|qq=}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|qq=}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|qq=}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
