{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|1=Yamada District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31514}} and {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi, Gunma|1=Maebashi}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|WD=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別|1=Aratawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216467}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359475}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|1=Kamitsukeno no Oguma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920670}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|1=Kamitsukeno no Katana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359474}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|1=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406951}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|1=Kaihito Kamitsukeno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359476}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野国造]]
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|1=Yamada District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31514}} and {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi, Gunma|1=Maebashi}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|WD=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別|1=Aratawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216467}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359475}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|1=Kamitsukeno no Oguma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920670}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|1=Kamitsukeno no Katana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359474}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|1=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406951}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|1=Kaihito Kamitsukeno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359476}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, "Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogies" (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * ''Japanese Historical Encyclopedia'' (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * ''Shintō Encyclopedia'' (Rinchosha)

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|1=Yamada District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31514}} and {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi, Gunma|1=Maebashi}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|WD=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別|1=Aratawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17216467}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209207}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359475}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|1=Kamitsukeno no Oguma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920670}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|1=Kamitsukeno no Katana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359474}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|1=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406951}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|1=Kaihito Kamitsukeno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359476}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.

{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633375}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581000}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487215}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1886428}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|qid=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|qid=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|1=Yamada District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q31514}} and {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|qid=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi, Gunma|1=Maebashi}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|qid=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qid=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別|1=Aratawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216467}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209207}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359475}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|1=Kamitsukeno no Oguma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27920670}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|1=Kamitsukeno no Katana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359474}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|1=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406951}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|1=Kaihito Kamitsukeno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359476}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |family_name=Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko |family_crest_image= |family_crest_name= |main_lineage={{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} |founder={{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633375}} |classification={{ill|Imperial descent|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial descent|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581000}} (alternative theories exist) |origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} |territory={{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}} |notable_figures=See [[#Figures]] section |branch_clans={{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|能等国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487215}} }}
'''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (上毛野国造, also read as Kamitsukeno Kuni no Miyatsuko or Kamitsukeno Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko) governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}}. Originally part of {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1886428}}, it was divided into {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|qid=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}.
== Overview ==
=== Names === The clan is referred to as '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi''' in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and '''Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Genealogy of the Nation").
=== Ancestry === * According to the ''Kojiki,'' {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命}} is the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clans|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. * The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, was appointed governor of the fifteen provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|qid=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō|1=Tōsandō}} under Emperor Keikō, with his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}} continuing to govern effectively. * The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' notes that Prince Toyokuni's grandson {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashima no Mikoto|lt=Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭嶋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the first to pacify twelve eastern provinces and was appointed as Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} (with the kabane title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|キミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to きみ}}) was responsible for governing the eastern provinces and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. Historically, the Kamitsukeno clan descended from Prince Toyokuni, who brought the worship of the {{ill|Izumo deity|ja|出雲神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the Keno region. The original Keno Province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (present-day Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno (present-day Tochigi Prefecture).
=== Territory === The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko's stronghold was likely located in the regions surrounding {{ill|Yamada District|ja|山田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada District|1=Yamada District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q31514}} and {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|qid=Q7041539|lt=Nitta District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}}. Numerous large Kofuns (kofun) in these areas are believed to have been built as their ancestral tombs.
== Domain == The Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed {{ill|Kamitsukeno Province|ja|上毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野国造|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}}, which corresponds to present-day Gunma Prefecture.
== Clan Shrine == The clan's {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is thought to be {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi, Gunma|1=Maebashi}}, which is dedicated to the deities {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Prince Toyokiirihiko|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
=== Tombs === * {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|qid=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonzan Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} (Tochigi Prefecture): A mid-4th-century {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 117 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto.<ref>Hoga, Hisao, "The Buried Kings of Japan," Aogaki Publishing, 2005.</ref> * {{ill|Asakozuka Kofun|ja|朝子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 123 meters. Possibly the tomb of Mimorowake no Mikoto's wife. * {{ill|Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|ja|別所茶臼山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture): A late 4th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 164 meters. Possibly the tomb of Aratowake. * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qid=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} (Gunma Prefecture): A 5th-century zenpō-kōhō-fun measuring 210 meters. Possibly the tomb of Takahase.
== Figures == * {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}: A statesman who governed the Keno region during Emperor Keikō's reign. * {{ill|Aratowake no Mikoto|ja|大荒田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒田別|1=Aratawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216467}}: A warrior active during the regency of Empress Jingū. * {{ill|Takahase no Mikoto|ja|竹葉瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahase no Mikoto|1=Takehase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209207}}: A warrior who served Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|ja|上毛野田道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Tadachi|1=Kamitsukeno no Tamichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359475}}: A military general under Emperor Nintoku. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Oguma|ja|上毛野小熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Oguma|1=Kamitsukeno no Oguma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27920670}}: A military leader active during Emperor Ankan's reign.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Katakana|ja|上毛野形名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Katakana|1=Kamitsukeno no Katana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359474}}: A general under Emperor Jomei. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}}: A general active during Emperor Tenji's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Michizane|ja|上毛野三千|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Michizane|1=Kamitsukeno no Michiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406951}}: A scholar during Emperor Tenmu's reign. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|ja|上毛野穎人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kakuto|1=Kaihito Kamitsukeno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359476}}: A court official during the Heian period. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja|上毛野滋子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Shigeko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A lady-in-waiting in the Heian court. * {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja|上毛野永世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Nagayo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A statesman involved in legal compilations during the Heian period.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
